title: DOCKER Container Port Mapping Validator with Port Collision

statement.markdown: Implement a function to validate a Docker container port mapping configuration. The configuration is a list of objects, each containing a service name and a list of port mappings. Each port mapping is an object with a container port and a host port. The function should return `true` if the configuration is valid and `false` otherwise. A configuration is valid if it does not contain any port collisions (i.e., multiple port mappings for the same container port).
statement.inputFormat: A list of objects, each containing a service name and a list of port mappings.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the configuration is valid.
statement.notes: The input configuration is not empty and contains at least one service.

constraints.description: Number of services in the configuration
Number of port mappings for each service

hints.text: Use a set to keep track of used container ports

editorial.title: Container Port Mapping Validator with Port Collision
editorial.intuition: The function should iterate over the port mappings for each service and check for collisions.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: check each port mapping against all other mappings for the same service.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a set to keep track of used container ports and check for collisions.
editorial.optimalSolution: Iterate over the port mappings for each service and use a set to check for collisions.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function returns true if there are no collisions and false otherwise.
